title: tanacetum kittaryanum subsp. uralense
intro: Tanacetum kittaryanum subsp. uralense, also known as Ural pyrethrum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The Ural pyrethrum has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and may also be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
